Ntc Of Mtn For Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending Appellate Review Of Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW J. SPIELBERGER, TEL. NO. (424)832-7677 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PATRICK E. TAYLOR, JR OF BRADY, VORWERCK, RYDER & CASPINO, TEL. NO. (925)274-9500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO;
ATTY. LAWRENCE R. RIFF & ATTY. JENNIFER BONNEVILLE (COURT CALL) OF STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 439-9494 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AMREP;
RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant/Cross-Complainant City and County of San Francisco's Motion For Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending Appellate Review Of Summary Judgment is Denied. The appeal of the grant of Parker Hannifin's motion for summary judgment does not automatically stay any portion of this case other than the City's claims against Parker Hannifin. The court declines to exercise its discretion to stay any other claims. A stay would significantly delay resolution of this case, which has already been pending over two years. The City has not shown that it is likely to be successful on its appeal. Even if the City is successful on its appeal, the resolution of plaintiff's claims against the City prior to the finalization of the appeal might eliminate any need for the City's claims against Parker Hannifin and, at the very least, would provide significant guidance as to the scope of those claims. Moreover, per CCP 437c(n)(2) and (3), the granting of summary judgment in favor of Parker Hannifin does not necessarily have any affect on any of the remaining claims by or against the City. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK
